audience_description: HQ Directors at Eduversal — Primary Schools Director and Secondary Schools Director. Sit above Subject Specialists and Coordinators. Own: network-wide academic strategy for their stage range, partner-school relationships at the principal/yayasan level, Specialist team performance, network annual cycle (Annual Workshop, EASE cycles, Sem-1+Sem-2 visit windows), reporting to Eduversal CEO/board, and (under EPDP) Principal Mentor role for newly appointed school principals.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikdasmen 7/2025 — Penugasan Guru sebagai Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Director engages with principal appointment regulation as Eduversal-side advisor to yayasans.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikbud 6/2018
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Director provides network-side supervisi function for partner-school principals.

regulatory_anchors.title: Permendikdasmen 13/2025 — Pembelajaran Mendalam
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Director leads network-wide implementation of deep learning approach.

regulatory_anchors.title: PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015 — 8 SNP
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Director holds network-wide accountability for the 8 SNP.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge Handbook for Centres
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Director is Eduversal-side primary contact with Cambridge International for partner Centres.

regulatory_anchors.title: Eduversal Indonesia — Network Standards
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Director is co-author and primary enforcer of network standards for their stage range.

framework_anchors.cambridge_school_leader_standards_2023: Director anchors network-wide CSL implementation across all 25 standards.
framework_anchors.principal_appraisal_v1: Director leads annual Principal Appraisal cycle for partner school principals (under EPDP).
framework_anchors.epdp_observation_rubric: Director qualified as principal observer (NN3 cert holder) — primary observer for first-year and developing principals.
framework_anchors.school_appraisal_framework: Director reviews and signs off all school_appraisals_v2 outputs from Specialists in their stage.

recurring_tasks.title: Weekly Specialist team huddle
recurring_tasks.description: 60-minute weekly meeting with Subject Specialists in your stage. Agenda: visit schedule, network signals, Cambridge updates, escalations.

recurring_tasks.title: Weekly review of Specialist briefs to you
recurring_tasks.description: Read all Specialist monthly briefs (Specialist task SS-C5-003) due to you each first Monday. Aggregate cross-subject patterns. Identify network-level signals warranting CEO escalation.

recurring_tasks.title: Weekly partner school principal touch-base
recurring_tasks.description: Brief contact (call, email, Slack) with at least 3 partner school principals each week. Rolling — every principal heard from monthly minimum. Topic: how is the school doing? Any HQ blockers?
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Don't only hear from principals when something is wrong. Build the relationship before the crisis. The principal who hears from you weekly is the one who calls you when something is breaking.

monthly_tasks.title: Monthly network-stage report to Eduversal CEO/Board
monthly_tasks.description: Aggregate of: school appraisal visit findings, Specialist team performance, principal-cohort health, EASE cycle data, Cambridge results trends, risks requiring board awareness.

monthly_tasks.title: Monthly Specialist 1:1 coaching
monthly_tasks.description: 30-minute 1:1 with each Specialist in your stage. Their IPDL goals, network-wide subject signals, professional development needs.

termly_tasks.title: Termly Cambridge International liaison
termly_tasks.description: Quarterly contact with Cambridge International (regional rep): network status, Centre news, Centre-conditional issues, planning advance discussions.

termly_tasks.title: Termly network-stage strategic review
termly_tasks.description: Internal Eduversal review: network-stage performance against annual targets, course corrections, resource reallocation between schools.

tasks.title: Lead Eduversal Annual School Leadership Workshop (your stage)
tasks.description: Principal architect of the annual workshop track for partner schools at your stage range. Year priorities, network direction, syllabus updates, principal cohort orientation.

tasks.title: Sign off all annual partner school RKT/RKAS (where Eduversal sign-off applies)
tasks.description: Eduversal-side review of partner school annual plans. Verify alignment with network priorities + Cambridge readiness.

tasks.title: Confirm Specialist visit schedule + Principal cohort assignments
tasks.description: All Sem-1 Specialist visits scheduled. Principal mentor pairings confirmed for first-year principals (under EPDP — must hold active principal_mentor_endorsement).

tasks.title: Lead network-stage QAR aggregation
tasks.description: Aggregate all partner-school QAR Findings Reports across your stage. Identify network-level signals. Brief Eduversal CEO. Direct any cross-school resource reallocation.

tasks.title: Approve EASE I network paper for your stage
tasks.description: Final sign-off on EASE I papers for your stage range. Cambridge-aligned, network-comparable, valid measurement.

tasks.title: Post-EASE I network analysis
tasks.description: Once results in: cross-school comparative analysis. Identify schools needing intensive support. Resource intervention decisions.

tasks.title: Co-deliver mid-year network leadership conference
tasks.description: Mid-year alignment with all partner-school leadership teams in your stage. EASE I analysis presented. Sem-2 priorities confirmed.

tasks.title: Oversee Sem-2 Specialist visit cycle
tasks.description: Same as Sem-1 — visit logistics, findings aggregation, escalations management. Specialists report to you weekly during the window.

tasks.title: Lead network-stage EASE II cycle
tasks.description: Same as EASE I, second cycle. Year-on-year comparison enables longitudinal network tracking.

38-39.phase: Late June — Annual Principal Appraisal cycle

tasks.title: Conduct annual Principal Appraisal for your principal cohort
tasks.description: Under EPDP: lead the formal Principal Appraisal v1.0 cycle for principals in your stage. F1 Cadence + F2 Observation + F3 360 + F4 Outcomes + F5 Self-Assessment composite. Sign off the recommendation triplet (continue / development plan / non-renewal). NN5-compliant: only the triplet is shared with yayasan + Foundation Rep — never item-level scores.

tasks.title: Annual network-stage report to Eduversal Board
tasks.description: Comprehensive year-end report: network-stage performance, principal cohort health, Cambridge results trends, partner-school relationship status, recommendations for next year.

tasks.title: Year-end Specialist team appraisals
tasks.description: Annual appraisal for each Subject Specialist in your stage. Review their year of school visits, network-level contributions, professional development. Set next-year goals.

tasks.title: Hand over to next year's intake
tasks.description: If a new Specialist joins next year, your year-end notes are their orientation foundation. Document network state, ongoing relationships, open issues.

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Quarterly mentoring with Eduversal CEO + outgoing Director
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: First-year Directors meet quarterly with CEO + outgoing Director (or peer Director if not available). Strategic orientation, network history, partner-school relationship handovers.

extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.title: Director certification — including principal_mentor_endorsement (NN3)
extra_tasks_first_12_weeks.description: Complete Eduversal Director certification within first 12 weeks. Includes principal_mentor_endorsement (Charter NN3) — required before assigning any Principal mentee.

provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge School Leader Standards 2023, principal_appraisal_v1 (when ratified), school-appraisal-framework.json, principal-observation-rubric.json
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Eduversal-network role definition. Director sits above Semesta's ALT — the network-level equivalent of a Semesta Principal but for 7-8 partner schools rather than 1.
